title: In-Flight Promise Deduplication, Waiters, Abort Ownership, and Cleanup · Contract Lens: Pokedex Ts Rejection Cleanup
summary: Concurrent misses create duplicate fetches, a shared promise retains one caller signal, first cancellation rejects all waiters, and settled entries remain in the in-flight map. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts rejection cleanup increment in waiter-aware duplicate-suppressed async loader. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Remove settled in-flight entries on success, validation failure, network failure, timeout, and abort without unhandled rejection.
Allow a waiter to stop observing without necessarily aborting work required by others and cancel shared work only under policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Pokedex Ts Rejection Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 4628 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one shared load, independent waiter abort, safe publication, rejection handling, and cleanup; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Remove settled in-flight entries on success, validation failure, network failure, timeout, and abort without unhandled rejection.
